notes: v1.14.50 - WORDMARK SWAP per PHANTOM_WORDMARK_HANDOFF (addendum to the entry handoff). Replaced the v1.14.49 Orbitron live-text PHANTOM wordmark with the embedded chrome wordmark IMAGE (phantom_only.webp, 1100x141 transparent, iridescent cyan->violet->magenta chrome + bevel + glow in alpha) INLINED as base64 data:image/webp (~104KB) over the dark entry bg - pixel-exact chrome a webfont can't do, and avoids embedding a commercial display font. Taglines stay LIVE Chakra Petch text (razor-sharp at DPR3, editable): eyebrow reworded 'Field Deployment System' -> 'Field Intelligence System' (matches the meta + the lockup art), sub 'Rack . Stack . Verify'; tagline color shifted to soft blue-violet (#aab6e8 / #8e9bd0) to match the chrome lockup. FIT (Rule 1 / the spec's priority): .pm-wordmark { display:block; width:min(82vw,360px); height:auto; margin:0 auto; aspect-ratio:1100/141 } - fluid with a 360px cap, aspect locked (no distortion), no overflow:hidden ancestor clipping the glow, reserves space to avoid layout shift. At 375pt width -> ~307px/39px tall; at 430pt -> ~352px/45px; both well within the viewport, centered, seated in the clean zone (~52% down) preserving the iris + CAL clearance. Optional shimmer-sweep reveal NOT added (spec said ship static first; defer behind a flag). Orbitron NOT unloaded (still referenced elsewhere); only the now-unused .pe-wordmark gradient rule was replaced by .pm-wordmark. No sw precache change (wordmark inlined like the globe). Gates: node --check 3/3, <style> braces 2655/2655, four stamps lockstep v1.14.50, dct/sw CRLF + version.json LF. DEVICE (John, iPhone, close+reopen to clear SW cache): verify the chrome wordmark renders transparent (no black box), glow intact (not clipped), centered + sharp + no overflow at BOTH ~375pt (SE/mini) and ~430pt (Pro Max); if the min(82vw,360px) cap reads too big/small on the OLED, that one number is the dial. STILL PENDING from v1.14.49: the ?cal=1 node-calibration pass (drag the 21 mesh nodes onto their cities, COPY the JSON back to bake locked coords).
